title: Trade Ideas Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026)
meta_title: Trade Ideas Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026) | TradingToolsHub
meta_description: Trade Ideas Complete Guide: Setup, Features & Tips (2026). Trade Ideas (4.3★, Free). Step-by-step setup, features walkthrough, and pro tips. Updated 2026.
excerpt: Complete guide to setting up and using Trade Ideas — from account creation to pro-level tips.
content:
What is Trade Ideas?

Trade Ideas is an AI-powered stock scanner built specifically for active traders and day traders who need high-probability setups before the market opens. Founded in 2003 and headquartered in Encinitas, California, the platform uses Holly AI—an algorithmic engine that backtests millions of technical patterns nightly to surface the most promising trading opportunities with real-time streaming alerts. It's rated 4.3 out of 5 stars by traders across all experience levels, and it's designed for traders who want to automate signal generation, eliminate guesswork, and execute faster. Trade Ideas offers a free tier ($0/month), a Standard plan at $127/month, and a Premium plan at $254/month, making it accessible for beginners to explore, but requiring a meaningful commitment for professional features like automated trading and Holly AI signals.

How to Create Your Trade Ideas Account

Creating a Trade Ideas account takes about 5 minutes and follows a straightforward process. Visit the Trade Ideas website and click "Sign Up." You'll be asked for your email address, a strong password, and your first and last name. Select your account type—individual trader, institutional, or paper trading—and proceed to email verification. Trade Ideas will send a confirmation link to your inbox; click it to activate your account.

Once verified, you'll be prompted to select a subscription plan. If you're new to the platform, choose the free tier (The Tester) to explore the dashboard and Holly AI alerts without spending money. No credit card is required for the free tier. If you decide to upgrade to Standard ($127/month) or Premium ($254/month), you'll need to provide billing information at that time. Trade Ideas accepts all major credit cards and PayPal. There are no additional verification requirements beyond email confirmation, so you can log in and start using the platform immediately after account activation.

Setting Up Trade Ideas for the First Time

After logging in, you'll see the Trade Ideas dashboard, which is divided into four main sections: the Holly AI Scanner (left panel), Stock Racing (center watchlist), the Alert Console (right panel), and the News Feed (bottom). On your first login, start by adjusting your timezone and market hours. Click Settings in the top right corner, then go to Preferences. Set your timezone to match your local trading hours so alerts fire at the correct times. Trade Ideas only scans during official market hours (9:30 AM – 4:00 PM ET for stocks), so this ensures you don't miss early alerts.

Next, configure your notification preferences. Click Alerts in the top menu and choose how you want to receive signals: in-app notifications (default), email, SMS, or Telegram. If you're a day trader, SMS and Telegram are critical—you can't miss Holly AI's pre-market signals while reading email. Set up at least one real-time notification method before you enable Holly AI. Then, customize the Holly AI parameters. Go to Holly AI Settings and adjust the confidence threshold (set to 75%+ if you're risk-averse, 50%+ if you want more opportunities). Select which market sectors or stock universes to scan—by default, it covers all U.S. stocks, but you can narrow to large-cap (Russell 1000) if you prefer lower-volatility setups. Finally, if you're using the free tier, pin the Holly AI widget to your dashboard so it's always visible. Premium users should also enable the Money Machine automated trading widget if they plan to use algorithmic execution.

Essential Features You Should Know

Holly AI Scanner: Trade Ideas' flagship feature runs millions of backtests every night and delivers a ranked list of high-probability trade setups each morning before the market opens. It analyzes 500+ technical data points per stock and returns only the setups with the highest statistical edge. You don't configure individual alerts; Holly AI does the work for you. On the free tier, you get access to Holly AI signals but with a 15-minute delay. Premium subscribers see signals 30+ minutes before market open, which is a significant trading advantage. Check the Holly AI watchlist first thing every morning and add any setups that match your risk profile to your trading plan.

Stock Racing: This visual momentum tracker displays stocks in real-time, sorted by intraday performance and technical momentum. Stocks appear as colored bars that lengthen or shrink as they move, making it instantly obvious which tickers are trending. Use Stock Racing during market hours to catch momentum shifts you might miss in a traditional price chart. It's especially useful for scalpers and momentum traders who need to enter and exit within minutes. You can filter by sector, change the time interval (1-minute to daily), and set percentage thresholds so only the strongest movers appear.

Money Machine (Premium Only): This is Trade Ideas' fully automated trading module that connects to your Interactive Brokers or E*TRADE account. Once enabled, Money Machine automatically executes trades based on Holly AI signals with built-in risk management (position size limits, max daily loss, profit targets). It respects your cash availability and won't over-leverage. For traders who want to remove emotion and execute on every signal without manual intervention, Money Machine is transformative. Configure position size as a percentage of your account (default is 2–5%), set your max daily loss tolerance, and let it trade. This feature is exclusive to the Premium plan at $254/month.

Backtesting Engine: Trade Ideas lets you test any custom strategy against historical price data. Go to the Backtest tab, define your entry and exit rules using the visual rule builder or code, and instantly see win rate, profit factor, and drawdown metrics over any date range. This is critical for validating that a trading idea actually works before risking real money. Run backtests on the same patterns Holly AI uses so you understand why certain setups have statistical edge.

Paper Trading: The free tier includes full paper trading (simulated trading with no real money). Execute trades on your Stock Racing watchlist in real money-like conditions, with realistic slippage and commissions, to build confidence before trading live. Track your paper P&L alongside the live market to see if your strategy actually works.

Channel Bar Templates: Trade Ideas comes with 30+ pre-built technical chart templates that save hours of workspace setup. These include channel breakout patterns, support/resistance confluence, moving average ribbons, and volatility bands. Apply any template to a chart instantly instead of manually drawing every indicator. Standard and Premium tiers have full access; free tier has a limited set.

Real-Time Alerts with 500+ Data Points: Beyond Holly AI, you can set custom alerts on any technical indicator: moving average crosses, RSI oversold, volume spikes, earnings announcements, and more. The alert engine streams data every few seconds, so you'll catch intraday momentum shifts instantly. Set alerts conservatively—too many false alarms will train you to ignore them.

Trade Ideas Pricing: Which Plan Should You Choose?

The Tester (Free, $0/month): You get access to Holly AI signals (15-minute delay), Stock Racing, basic paper trading, 30+ Channel Bar templates, and the backtesting engine with limited historical data. This is perfect if you're a beginner exploring whether AI-driven trading suits your style or if you want to validate Holly AI signals with paper trading before committing money. The free tier is fully functional for learning, but the 15-minute delay makes real-time trading impossible.

Blue Bundle / Standard ($127/month): Removes the 15-minute Holly AI delay (signals now come 30+ minutes before market open), adds unlimited historical backtesting, and includes broker integration for paper trading across more accounts. This tier is ideal for semi-active traders who want Holly AI's edge without paying for full automation. The Standard plan is the most popular tier and offers the best price-to-feature ratio. At roughly $3 per trading day, it's a reasonable cost if Holly AI helps you catch two or three winning setups per week.

Powder Elite / Premium ($254/month): Includes everything in Standard, plus Money Machine (automated trading), priority alerts, advanced integrations with Interactive Brokers and E*TRADE for live trading, and exclusive premium-tier signals. At $254/month ($12+ per trading day), this tier is justified only if you're (1) already profiting consistently and need to scale, (2) trading full-time, or (3) value the psychological advantage of automated execution. Most retail traders should start with Standard and upgrade only after proving Holly AI's signals work for their strategy.

For most traders in 2026, the Standard plan at $127/month is the sweet spot—you get real Holly AI signals in real-time, unlimited backtesting, and the ability to validate the platform's edge before committing to automation. If you're day trading and Holly AI becomes central to your strategy, Premium ($254/month) is still cheaper than paying a prop trading firm's desk fees.

Pro Tips for Getting the Most Out of Trade Ideas

1. Backtest Every Holly AI Pattern Before Trading It Live: Don't take a signal at face value just because Holly AI provided it. Use the backtesting engine to run that specific pattern against the last 5 years of historical data and confirm it has a win rate above 50% and a profit factor above 1.5x. This single discipline will save you thousands in false signals and eliminate trades you don't psychologically trust.

2. Run Holly AI Scans in Market Hours, Not Just Pre-Market: While the pre-market Holly AI list is the headline feature, enable intraday scanning as well. Go to Holly AI Settings and turn on mid-day scans (9:30 AM, 12:00 PM, 2:00 PM ET). Intraday setups won't be as rigorously backtested, but they catch momentum that emerged after market open—especially useful if you're a day trader or swing trader.

3. Use Stock Racing to Confirm Holly AI Signals in Real-Time: When Holly AI alerts you to a setup, don't enter immediately. Switch to Stock Racing, find that ticker, and watch its momentum bar for 2–3 minutes. If the bar is lengthening (momentum confirming) and the stock is trading above key moving averages, enter. If the bar is shrinking, wait or skip the trade. This simple overlay step reduces whipsaw entries by 30%.

4. Set Broker Integration Correctly or You'll Miss Automated Execution: If you're on the Premium plan and want Money Machine to work, make sure your Interactive Brokers or E*TRADE credentials are properly synced. Go to Account Connections, authorize the broker, and run a test trade (buy 1 share of a liquid stock, then close it) to confirm the link works. Many new Premium users enable Money Machine but skip this step and wonder why no trades execute.

5. Create a Watchlist of Holly AI Rejects to Find Contrarian Setups: Holly AI's strength is identifying high-probability patterns, but sometimes the stocks it rejects set up beautifully for mean-reversion plays. Create a separate watchlist called "Contrarian Thesis" and manually add stocks Holly AI passed on that meet your reverse setup criteria. Track these over time and measure their win rate—you might find an edge Holly AI missed.

6. Monitor Your Win Rate Weekly, Not Daily: Day trading has natural noise; a 40% win rate with a 3:1 reward-to-risk ratio is profitable. Don't abandon Holly AI after three losing days. Run a weekly backtest on all the trades you took (both executed and simulated) and measure your cumulative Sharpe ratio. If it's positive over 20+ trades, the system is working. If it's negative, adjust your parameters or position size.

7. Whitelist Your Email Domain So Holly AI Alerts Don't Land in Spam: Trade Ideas alerts come from noreply@tradeideas.com. Add this address to your email contacts and ask your email provider to whitelist it. A pre-market Holly AI signal stuck in spam is a missed $500 trade—this takes 30 seconds to prevent and is non-negotiable.

Common Trade Ideas Issues and How to Fix Them

Issue: Holly AI Alerts Arrive but You Miss Them Because Notifications Aren't Working: Solution: Trade Ideas' notification system requires explicit browser permissions. When you first log in, your browser will ask "Allow notifications from Trade Ideas?" Click Allow. If you missed this or clicked Deny, go to your browser settings (Chrome: Settings → Privacy → Site Settings → Notifications, find tradeideas.com, and set it to Allow). Also enable SMS or Telegram as a backup notification method in Alert Preferences—email alone is too slow for day trading.

Issue: Backtesting Results Look Great but Live Trades Lose Money: Solution: This is usually due to slippage and commissions not being accurately modeled in your backtest. In the Backtest settings, increase the slippage assumption from the default 0.05% to 0.15% and set commissions to match your broker's actual fees (most brokers charge $0.01 per share for stocks). Run the backtest again. If the strategy is still profitable, it's likely a good signal. If it becomes unprofitable, the strategy is too tight for real execution and you should skip it.

Issue: Money Machine Trades Are Executing but in the Wrong Position Size or at the Wrong Price: Solution: Go to Preferences and confirm your account equity is correctly synced to Trade Ideas. Money Machine calculates position size as a percentage of your account value; if Trade Ideas thinks you have $10,000 when you actually have $50,000, position sizes will be 5x too small. Also check that your Interactive Brokers or E*TRADE API permissions are set to "Full Trading" (not just "View Only" or "Alerts Only").

Issue: Stock Racing Freezes or Lags During Market Hours: Solution: This typically means your internet connection is saturated or your browser has too many tabs open. Trade Ideas streams real-time data and can consume significant bandwidth. Close all non-essential tabs, ensure your internet speed is at least 10 Mbps download, and restart your browser. If the problem persists, refresh the Trade Ideas page (Ctrl+R or Cmd+R) or contact Trade Ideas support—it may be a platform-wide issue.

Is Trade Ideas Worth It? Our Verdict

Trade Ideas is worth the $127/month Standard plan if you're a day trader or active swing trader who wants Holly AI's pre-market signal edge and has already validated the platform with paper trading. At 4.3 out of 5 stars, it's one of the few scanners built specifically for algorithmic signal generation rather than manual charting, and Holly AI's nightly backtesting is genuinely differentiated from competitors like TradingView and Finviz. The $254/month Premium plan is overkill unless you're a full-time trader or ready to deploy Money Machine automation. Where Trade Ideas falls short: the desktop interface feels dated, there's no mobile app (critical for traders who want flexibility), and the shared Holly AI signals across all Premium subscribers can create crowded-trade slippage when everyone gets the same alert at once. Start with the free tier, graduate to Standard if Holly AI helps you win more trades, and skip Premium unless automation becomes central to your strategy. For day traders serious about AI-driven setups, Trade Ideas is one of your best options in 2026.
author: TradingToolsHub Editorial
